page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high-performance computing instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system lacks hyperthreading, allocating one thread per physical core to ensure dedicated compute resources. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 10% of all servers. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations achieving top-tier results. The processor's support for AVX-512 and advanced vector extensions contributes to top-tier performance in Passmark CPU physics and extended instruction tests. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% across stress-ng and Geekbench evaluations.

This instance is designed for compute-intensive and memory-bandwidth-bound workloads such as high-performance computing, molecular modeling, fluid dynamics, and software compilation, where its Clang compilation benchmark ranks in the top 10%. It also demonstrates strong capabilities for database operations and Redis SET operations, which reached 122.53 million operations per second. However, the lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage, and the absence of dedicated GPU hardware makes it less suitable for heavy GPU-accelerated deep learning, though it maintains strong to average CPU-based LLM inference speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is an x86_64 high-performance computing instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core and uncached read/write operations ranking in the top 10%. It excels in compute-heavy and memory-bound tasks, such as software compilation, physics simulations, and database operations. The lack of local storage and dedicated GPUs represents a tradeoff, making it best suited for clustered HPC workloads, large-scale simulations, and memory-intensive parallel processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge features 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 150 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for high-performance computing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high-performance computing instance with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 150 Gbps networking.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute node featuring massive DDR5 memory bandwidth and high-speed networking for parallel scientific and engineering workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical AMD EPYC cores with no hyperthreading
768 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 16 GB per core
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Optimized for high-performance computing, simulations, and software compilation
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
